title: bee cells schedule CLI verb: registry entry + handler + usage fallback + examples
action: Per D1: add verb cells.schedule following the cells.ready pattern exactly. COMMAND_REGISTRY entry (both command-registry.mjs mirror copies): name cells.schedule, invoke 'bee cells schedule', description 'Compute the wave schedule for a feature: dep layering + file-overlap serialization, with cycle/unsatisfiable-dep/empty-files diagnostics.', parameters {feature: string (optional), json: boolean}, examples ['bee cells schedule --json']. Feature resolution follows handleCellsReady exactly: flags.feature ? String(flags.feature) : null (null = all cells) — NO state.json feature fallback (plan-checker W3 resolution). Handler handleCellsSchedule in templates/bee.mjs AND .bee/bin/bee.mjs (the two dispatchers are separate copies kept byte-identical by hand — the verify's diff clause proves it): load cells via listCells (filtered by feature or all), call computeSchedule from lib/schedule.mjs, print human-readable waves + diagnostics, --json emits the raw structure. Register in HANDLERS map; append 'schedule' to the hand-maintained cellsUsageFallback verb list (bee.mjs:1669-1672). test_bee_cli.mjs auto-runs registry examples in its temp repo — ensure the example passes there (empty store => empty waves, exit 0). Compat proof (owned by this cell): after implementing, run node .bee/bin/bee.mjs cells schedule --json against THIS repo's real store (200+ cells) — it must exit 0 with zero cycles; attach the output as cap evidence.

truths: bee cells schedule --json exits 0 on an empty/zero-cell store and prints valid JSON
the verb appears in the registry, the HANDLERS map, and the cells usage-fallback string — all three
waves match computeSchedule output exactly (no CLI-side re-sorting or filtering)
registry mirror copies stay byte-identical and the two dispatcher copies stay byte-identical (proven by the diff clause in verify)
the real store of this repo schedules clean: exit 0, zero cycles (output attached as cap evidence)
artifacts: registry entries in both command-registry.mjs copies
real-store schedule output in cap evidence
prohibitions: a new top-level command group (must live under cells — CONTEXT Agent's Discretion constraint)
disk writes from the schedule verb (read-only)
a state.json feature fallback in the handler

trace.outcome: Added bee cells schedule CLI verb (D1): registry entry + handleCellsSchedule handler in both dispatcher copies + HANDLERS map + cellsUsageFallback, read-only pass-through of computeSchedule's waves/diagnostics, feature resolution matches handleCellsReady (no state.json fallback). Compat-proven against this repo's real 206+ cell store: exit 0, zero cycles, waves match plan.md's predicted slice-1 schedule exactly.
